expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: heatmap highlighting effect scenarios cover activation, persistence, and reset behavior
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the focus as: "heatmap highlighting effect scenarios covering activation, persistence, reset behavior" and includes scenarios for activation ("Tap a single heatmap cell activates highlight styling", "Tap a different cell moves highlight"), persistence ("Scroll ... does not unintentionally clear highlight", "Switch to another app and return; highlight persists", "Rotate device"), and reset ("Tap the currently highlighted cell again resets highlight", "Tap outside ... clears highlight", "Apply a filter ... highlight resets appropriately").

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled "Phase 4a (Subcategory-only) QA draft coverage" and includes a "Phase 4a alignment (contract check)" section stating the required structure and "must not use canonical top-layer categories". The plan uses a subcategory-only structure under "Heatmap highlight effect (iOS)" with scenario → action chain → observable verification leaves, and does not introduce top-layer groupings like Security/Compatibility/EndToEnd.
